B: People from Tuyen Quang don't call their parents "bố mẹ".

standard: Hẳn là hai lần mà vẫn chưa được ảnh nào à chế?
A: So, you've tried twice and still haven't gotten a single photo, huh?
B: It must have been twice already, but you still haven't gotten any photos, right?
C: Surely two times but still no photos yet, huh?

standard: Ai về Chợ Mới An Giang không nhỉ!
A: Is anyone going to Cho Moi, An Giang?
B: Is anyone going back to Cho Moi, An Giang?
C: Is anyone going back to Chợ Mới, An Giang?

standard: thương quá mẹ ạ, không khổ nào bằng.
A: Oh, Mom, I feel so sorry for you. There's no suffering like this.
B: I feel so much pity, Mom; there is no suffering worse than this.
C: I love you so much, Mom. No suffering compares to this.

standard: Nước đó nghèo khủng khiếp có quả cam cũng bâu lại chia.
A: That country was terribly poor; they would even huddle together to share an orange.
B: That country is terribly poor; even if there is an orange, they all crowd around to share it.
C: That country is extremely poor, even when they have an orange, they still gather together to share it.

standard: hôm nào bạn xuống dắt bạn đi ăn nhé
A: When you come down here sometime, let's go out to eat.
B: Whenever you come down, I'll take you out to eat.
C: Someday when you come down, take me out to eat, okay?

standard: Cái này hồi mấy bữa trước khai trương, chứ giờ còn giá đó đâu.
A: This was when they first opened a few days ago; the price isn't that low anymore.
B: This was from a few days ago during the grand opening; it's not that price anymore.
C: This was from when they opened a few days ago—that price doesn't exist anymore now.

standard: Ai muốn đi thì đi bớt cho Việt Nam nó rộng. Chật quá rồi
A: Those who want to leave should leave so Vietnam can have more space. It's too crowded already.
B: Whoever wants to leave should just go so that Vietnam can have more space. It's too crowded already.
C: If anyone wants to leave, just go so Vietnam has more space. It's gotten too cramped already.

standard: Vậy 5 tạ là bao nhiêu chục yến nhỉ?
A: So, how many tens of yens is 5 quintals?
B: So how many tens of yến is 5 quintals?
C: So how many dozens of yến is 5 tạ?

standard: Tui khoái nhất vụ này này, nhớ lại vẫn thấy vui
A: This is my favorite part; I still feel happy remembering it.
B: I love this part the most; remembering it still makes me happy.
C: I love this thing the most, I still find it fun when I recall it

standard: nghe thôi, chứ mày không được ăn Tết rồi
A: Just listen, but you won't get to celebrate Tet (Vietnamese New Year).
B: Just listen, because you won't be able to celebrate Tet.
C: Just listen, because you won't get to celebrate Tet anymore

standard: nhà chị Hương là gần nhất chị nhỉ, tiện thật đấy?
A: Ms. Huong's house is the closest, isn't it? That's really convenient!
B: Ms. Huong's house is the closest, isn't it? That's really convenient.
C: Chị Hương's house is the closest, right? That's really convenient, isn't it?

standard: Em bò đã không biết rồi, chứ nói gì đến bứt cỏ.
A: The cow didn't know how to do it, let alone pull up grass.
B: I didn't even know about the cow, let alone pulling up the grass.
C: If the one who crawls doesn't even know, how could they pull grass?

standard: Quen qua mạng này nhé, gặp rồi có hết hồn không. Hay mất hồn luôn rồi?
A: We met online, right? Were you shocked when we met? Or were you completely stunned?
B: We met online, right? Now that we've met, are you shocked? Or have you completely lost your soul?
C: Let's get to know each other online, okay? Will we be completely blown away when we meet? Or have we already lost our minds?

standard: Ngồi đấy mà bình yên, ngồi tụm lại là nhao nhác như cái chợ.
A: Sitting there is peaceful, but gathering in groups makes it as chaotic as a marketplace.
B: Sit there and act all peaceful, but as soon as you gather together, it's as chaotic as a market.
C: Sitting alone is peaceful, but sitting together is chaotic like a market.

standard: bà ấy là ca sĩ hay gì ấy lên YouTube tìm đi.
A: She's a singer or something like that, go search for her on YouTube.
B: She's a singer or something like that, go look for her on YouTube.
C: She might be a singer or something, go look her up on YouTube.

standard: được quá nhỉ.
A: That's great!
B: That's quite good, isn't it?
C: That's great!

standard: ăn còn hít hà khen ngon nữa chứ chúng mày.
A: They even kept sniffing and praising how delicious it was while eating.
B: I even inhaled the aroma and praised how delicious it was, you guys.
C: I'm eating and still going "mm mm" praising how delicious it is, you guys.

standard: mai dẫn tụi tôi tới chỗ này kiếm 4 triệu đi ăn nhé?
A: Mai, take us to this place so we can get 4 million VND to eat, okay?
B: Tomorrow, take us to this place to get 4 million to go eat, okay?
C: Can you take us to this place tomorrow to make 4 million [dong] and go eat?

standard: Câu trả lời là Hà Nội ạ.
A: The answer is Hanoi.
B: The answer is Hanoi.
C: The answer is Hanoi.

standard: Ước mơ chỉ là mơ thôi, không biết khi nào mới được thế này.
A: Dreams are just dreams; who knows when things will ever turn out like this?
B: Dreams are just dreams; I don't know when I'll ever be like this.
C: Dreams are just dreams, I don't know when I'll ever get to be like this.

standard: Ngon nhưng đắt quá nên thôi không ăn luôn.
A: It was delicious, but too expensive, so I decided not to eat it.
B: It's delicious, but too expensive, so I won't eat it.
C: It's delicious but way too expensive, so I just won't eat it at all.

standard: 2 vợ chồng xem làm sao mà tranh thủ đấy nhé.
A: The two of you should see how you can make the most of this opportunity.
B: The two of you should see how you can make the most of the time.
C: You two as a couple, try to make time for it, alright?

standard: Cô này nhìn vừa điêu mà lại vừa dữ nhỉ?
A: This woman looks both cunning and fierce, doesn't she?
B: This woman looks both deceitful and fierce, doesn't she?
C: This woman looks both fake and fierce, huh?

standard: Kêu Hùng vào nữa, ta làm một bữa lễ

B: Tell Hung to come in too, let's have a feast.
C: Invite Hùng in as well, let's prepare a feast.

standard: dùng với bún nước lèo là tuyệt vời.
A: It's excellent when served with noodle soup.
B: It is wonderful when used with bún nước lèo.
C: It's wonderful with bún nước lèo.

standard: Chú nào mà tóc hất qua 1 bên thế này
A: Which guy has his hair swept to one side like this?
B: Which guy has his hair swept to one side like this?
C: Which guy has his hair swept to one side like this

standard: Làm một chén muối ớt để chấm với mảng cầu là hết ý!
A: Making a bowl of chili salt dip to go with the custard apple is absolutely fantastic!
B: Making a bowl of chili salt to dip with custard apple is just perfect!
C: Making a bowl of salt and chili for dipping with fried dough sticks is just perfect!

standard: Ảnh thì không chụp, cũng không biết chụp ảnh, rủ đi làm gì.
A: He doesn't take photos, doesn't know how to take photos, so why invite me along?
B: I don't take photos, nor do I know how to take them, so why invite me?
C: I don't take photos, and I also don't know how to take photos. Why invite me to go?

standard: Cố gắng lên tất cả vì miền Bắc thân yêu, bọn chúng ăn ốc nói mò thì biết gì đâu.
A: Let's all try our best for our beloved North; those guys who talk nonsense know nothing.
B: Keep fighting, everyone, for our beloved North; they are just talking nonsense, so they don't know anything.
C: Keep it up, everyone, for our beloved North. They're just talking nonsense anyway, what do they know.

standard: Đèn giao thông đếm giây bữa nay bỏ chứ lỗi gì
A: The traffic lights with countdown timers are out today, what's the problem?
B: Why were the traffic light countdown timers removed today, what's the problem?
C: The countdown traffic lights were removed today? What's wrong with that?

standard: Có khi nào đi Vinh đâu mà biết.
A: I've never been to Vinh, so how would I know?
B: I've never been to Vinh, so how would I know.
C: How would I know? I've never been to Vinh.

standard: Thế đưa bớt tiền đây tiêu với, dùng sao hết ạ?
A: Give me some of your money to spend, how can I possibly use it all up?
B: Then give me some of that money to spend; how could you possibly use it all?
C: So give me some money here to spend together, how should we use it all?

standard: Dân quê vào thành phố học thôi
A: People from the countryside come to the city to study.
B: Just a country person coming to the city to study.
C: Just a villager heading to the city to study.

standard: Rầu khủng khiếp chúng mày ơi chúng mày.
A: I'm terribly sad, guys.
B: I'm terribly depressed, you guys.
C: I'm so terribly sad, you guys.

standard: xem sao tìm một thằng cho em nó đi chơi giao thừa.
A: Let's see if we can find a guy for her to go out with on New Year's Eve.
B: Let's see if I can find a guy for her to go out with on New Year's Eve.
C: Let me see, find a guy for my younger sibling to go out celebrating New Year's Eve with.

standard: Trả chứ tao không lấy, con rể tặng thì lấy chứ của chúng mày lấy làm gì
A: I'll give it back, I'm not taking it. If my son-in-law gave it to me, I'll take it, but what would I do with what you guys would take?
B: I'll return it because I'm not taking it; if my son-in-law gave it, I'd take it, but why would I take it from you guys?
C: Give it back then, I won't take it. If the son-in-law gives it, I'll take it. Why would I take yours?

standard: Ê chúng mày, đi đi chúng mày, biển ở trên đấy đẹp lắm.
A: Hey guys, let's go, the beach up there is beautiful.
B: Hey guys, go there, the beach up there is beautiful.
C: Hey guys, let's go, you guys, the beach up there is really beautiful.

standard: Gì mà khổ vậy
A: Why are you suffering so much?
B: Why is it so miserable?
C: Why is it this hard?

standard: Nhường em đó, may mà chị có chồng rồi không thì không đến lượt em đâu.
A: I'll let you have it. Luckily, I'm married, otherwise it wouldn't be your turn.

B: Adjusting the colors makes it look completely different, looks great.

B: What do you mean by "center"? Do I have to run into the forest to take the train?

C: Who even asked that a deputy minister had to answer?

B: If I threw this cake at my ex's face, people in Hanoi would probably hear it, wouldn't they?

A: I'm just asking to see if anyone's inviting me, you know.
B: I asked that just to see if anyone would invite me, you idiot.
C: I'm asking like that just to see if someone invited me or not, that's all.

A: This fish, when braised in a rich sauce with pork cracklings and pepper, tastes absolutely amazing.
B: This fish is great when braised with pork cracklings and pepper.

C: Never mind what you two think, but until the uncles and aunts go and do the formal introduction, that's how it is for now.

C: Sometimes after I finish driving, he makes sudden illegal turns and then curses at me.

A: I'm 50 years old and I remember hearing from a young age, when I went to buy wine for my father, the seller would tell me to give him the bottle and she would pour the wine for me.

C: I am 50 years old. Since I was a child, I've been going to buy wine for my dad, and the seller would tell me to hand over the bottle so she could pour the wine for me.

A: He's so sad, I don't know when the sun will come out so we can make a date.
B: Good grief, it just keeps raining; I don't know when it'll be sunny so I can make an appointment.
C: The weather has been so gloomy, I don't know when it'll be sunny to make plans.

A: We're still several kilometers behind Taiwan on this side.
B: It's many more kilometers than Taiwan over here.
C: Taiwan is many kilometers away on this side

A: The leech latches on and runs away in terror.
B: Once a leech attaches, I'll run for my life.

A: May I ask who did what to cause this breaking point?
B: May I ask who did what that caused the situation to reach a breaking point?
C: I'm asking who did what that made them reach their boiling point like that?

A: My sampuche (a type of fruit) is ripe and abundant. Please support me, everyone! My family can't eat it all.

C: Sampuche at my place is a lot. Please support me. Because my household can't finish eating it all.

A: I'm itching to go out, but I'm too busy right now.
B: I'm itching to go, but I'm busy right now and can't.
C: I'm feeling restless and really want to go already, but I'm busy right now so I can't.

A: Why did you do yoga at the wedding? Tell me.
B: Why do you do yoga at a wedding? Tell me about it.

B: The plan to go to the stream the other day accidentally fell through, sister.
C: I missed the plan to go to that stream the other day, sis.

standard: sợ nhỉ, công nhận mình lái xe siêu thiệt.
A: That's scary, I have to admit I'm a really good driver.
B: Scary, right? I have to admit, I'm a super driver.
C: Scary, I have to admit my driving is really good.

standard: xóa bớt dữ liệu đi bạn.
A: Please delete some of the data.
B: Delete some of the data.
C: Please delete some data.

standard: Giờ chỉ có đưa hai chân ra mà đi thôi, chứ chèo họ tưởng điên đấy.
A: Now you just have to walk on your feet; if you try to paddle, they'll think you're crazy.
B: Now I can only walk on my two feet, because if I paddle, they'll think I'm crazy.
C: Now I just have to put my feet out and go, otherwise if I keep pestering them they'll think I'm crazy.

standard: Làm gì đến 1 mét. Cơ quan nhà nước phân.
A: It's not even 1 meter. It's allocated by the government agency.
B: What do you mean by 1 meter? The state agency distributed it.
C: What is there to do up to 1 meter. State agencies distribute.

standard: Ê món này ưa thích thật luôn nhé!
A: Wow, this is my favorite dish!
B: Hey, I really love this dish!
C: Man, I really love this dish!

standard: Ừ nhỉ. Đang xăng lại pha nhớt thì toi.
A: Yeah, right. Mixing gasoline with oil would be disastrous.
B: Yeah, you're right. It would be terrible if I mixed the oil into the gas right now.
C: Yeah, right. Getting gas but then there's oil mixed in, and it's all ruined.

standard: Trời ơi. Bảo sao bữa giờ công an đứng đầy đường luôn.
A: Oh my God. No wonder there have been police officers everywhere on the streets lately.
B: Oh my god. No wonder the police have been all over the streets lately.
C: Oh my god. No wonder there have been police officers standing all over the streets lately.

standard: Ở chỗ mình gọi là canh rau rịa, nấu với cái gì cũng ngon hết, món ưa thích địa phương mình
A: In my area, we call it "rau rịa soup," and it tastes good with anything you cook it with; it's a favorite local dish.
B: Where I'm from, it's called canh rau rịa; it tastes delicious no matter what it's cooked with, and it's my local favorite dish.
C: Where I'm from we call it canh rau rịa, it's delicious with anything, and it's my favorite local dish.

standard: Bà có về không mà bà hay bàn quá vây.
A: Is she even coming home? She talks so much about it.
B: Are you even coming back, or why are you talking so much?
C: Are you going home or not, that you're always commenting too much?

standard: Hà Tĩnh ở chỗ nào thế nhỉ?
A: Where exactly is Ha Tinh located?
B: Where is Ha Tinh located?
C: Where exactly is Hà Tĩnh?

standard: Ngon không em? Để hôm nào về chị còn ăn thử này.
A: Is it delicious? I'll try some when I get home.
B: Is it good? I'll try it when I go back some other day.
C: Is it good, [younger person]? When I come back, I'll try this one.

standard: Bảo hiểm lần này lãnh đủ bạn nhỉ?
A: This time, the insurance company paid the full amount, didn't they?
B: The insurance payout this time is in full, right?
C: Got the full insurance payout this time, right?

standard: Mua đi, trà chỗ này ngon mà 20 nghìn thôi
A: Buy some, the tea here is delicious and only 20,000 VND.
B: Buy it, the tea here is delicious and it's only 20 thousand.
C: Just buy it, this tea here is delicious and only 20,000 dong.

standard: Cây này mọc khá lâu rồi. May mà không có thiệt hại về người.
A: This tree has been growing for quite a while. Luckily, there were no casualties.
B: This tree has been growing for quite a while. Fortunately, there were no casualties.
C: This tree has been there for quite a while. Fortunately, no one was injured.

standard: Đi nữa không, chứ mới 2 lần chứ mấy

B: Want to go again? It's only been twice, after all.

standard: Ủa vậy là sao ta?
A: Huh, what does that mean?
B: Wait, how does that work?
C: Wait, so what do you mean?

standard: nói không xuể, cả 1 rỗ đây.
A: There are too many to list, it's a whole basketful.
B: I can't even list them all, there's a whole basket of them here.
C: I can't even describe it all, there's a whole tray here.

standard: Tiền tỷ ở đâu mà thấy nhiều quá nhỉ?
A: Where did all that billions of dong come from?
B: Where did all these billions come from? There seems to be so much.
C: Where does one even find so many billions, I wonder?

standard: Rót nước vào cái chuyên.

B: Pour water into the container.
C: Pour water into the pot.

standard: Mà bạn này là trai hay gái vậy, sao không thấy cái ảnh đại diện nào hết vậy nhỉ
A: Is this person a boy or a girl? Why is there no profile picture?
B: But is this person a guy or a girl, and why aren't there any profile pictures?
C: But is this person a boy or a girl, why can't I see any profile picture at all

standard: Anh cút đi. Không làm gì được hết. Ngoài việc muốn em lấy vợ ra, anh muốn gì nữa?
A: Get out of here. There's nothing you can do. Besides wanting me to get married, what else do you want?
B: Get lost. You can't do anything. Besides wanting me to get married, what else do you want?
C: Get lost. You can't do anything anyway. Besides wanting me to get married, what else do you want?

standard: Bốc phét chứ uống được bao nhiêu.
A: He's just bragging; how much could he possibly drink?
B: You're bragging; how much can you actually drink?
C: Just talking big, but how much can you actually drink?

standard: Vậy chắc nhà tao nghèo chúng mày ơi chúng mày.
A: So, I guess my family is poor, you guys.
B: So my family must be poor, you guys.
C: So my family must be poor, you guys.

standard: Trời này ăn gì thì không cần bảo mọi người cũng biết rồi còn gì nữa
A: Everyone already knows what to eat in this weather, don't they?
B: Everyone already knows what to eat in this weather, so there's no need to tell them.
C: There's no need to tell anyone what to eat in this weather, everyone already knows anyway.

A: Tet (Vietnamese New Year) is so peaceful, isn't it?
B: It's so quiet and peaceful when Tet arrives, isn't it?
C: Tết is here and everything is so peaceful and quiet.

standard: Như này thì không mất một giọt tiền xăng để cưa gái.
A: This way, you won't spend a single drop of gas trying to impress a girl.
B: Like this, I won't spend a single drop of gasoline to woo girls.
C: This way, you don't need to spend even a drop of gas money to flirt with girls.

standard: Tôi nhìn giống bạn, ngồi lo hồi sáng giờ đây.
A: I look just like you, I've been sitting here worrying all morning.
B: I look just like you, sitting here worrying since this morning.
C: I look like you, sitting here worrying since this morning.

standard: chẳng lẽ tao bắt xe về tối nhậu với bọn mày.
A: Should I take a taxi home tonight to drink with you guys?
B: Am I really supposed to take a ride back just to drink with you guys tonight?
C: Don't tell me I have to take a ride back just to go drinking with you guys tonight.

standard: Mua bánh ướt ram nhé, dưa mùng nữa.
A: Buy some steamed rice rolls with fried shallots, and pickled mustard greens too.
B: Buy some fried steamed rice rolls and some pickled watermelon too.
C: Let's buy bánh ướt ram, and some pickled mùng tơi too.

standard: Tôi thấy sợ con người anh rồi đấy anh à.
A: I'm starting to get scared of you, man.
B: I'm starting to be afraid of who you are.
C: I'm really starting to get scared of you now, honestly.

standard: Nhà thì đông con. Ruộng thì làm có hơn một sào. Mà toàn vác miệng đi ăn nhà hàng 3 sao 5 sao cả nên có dư mấy bao lúa này.
A: We have a large family. We only cultivate a little over one sao (approximately 1000 square meters) of rice field. But we spend all our time eating at 3-star and 5-star restaurants, so we have a few sacks of rice left over.
B: The house is full of children. We only have a bit more than one sào of land. Yet, we're always out eating at 3-star and 5-star restaurants, so we only have a few bags of rice left over.
C: We have many children at home. We only work a little over one sào of field. But everyone just goes out to eat at 3-star, 5-star restaurants, so we ended up with this surplus of several bags of rice.

standard: Xã này lạ nhỉ?
A: This village is strange, isn't it?
B: This commune is strange, isn't it?
C: This commune is weird, huh?

standard: Bữa mà biết mình vào nhà cái nhỉ? Cho biết con rể cái.
A: If only I knew I was going to the casino, I'd let you know who my son-in-law is.
B: The day you find out I've entered the house, right? Let me be known as the son-in-law.
C: If only I had known to go to this one's house, right? Tell me about this one's son-in-law.

standard: Thôi, ngang đâu thì hưởng ngang đó, mấy anh này mình chưa đủ phước
A: Well, let's just accept what we have; I'm not lucky enough to have these guys yet.
B: Forget it, if it's meant to be, it's meant to be; I don't have enough merit for these guys.
C: Forget it, whatever level you're at, you enjoy at that level. These guys, I haven't had enough merit.

standard: món ưa thích em nữa đó.
A: That's another one of my favorites.
B: That's another one of my favorite dishes.
C: That's my favorite dish too.

standard: Chưa làm thế này thì có được mãi mãi không. Chứ tôi thấy tôi thèm lắm rồi ấy!
A: If we don't do this, will it ever work out? Because I'm really craving it!
B: If I don't do this, will it last forever? Because I'm really craving it now!
C: If I don't do this, will I get to have it forever? But I'm really craving it already!

standard: Chơi gì mà ác tởn vậy chúng mày?
A: What kind of cruel game are you guys playing?
B: What are you guys playing that's so cruel?
C: What are you guys playing at being so mean like that?

standard: Chốt ngày nào mời Thắng nhỉ?
A: When should we invite Thang?
B: Which date should we settle on to invite Thang?
C: Which day should we invite Thắng?

standard: Nhanh làm gì mà khổ vậy không biết
A: Why rush when you can do it so badly?
B: I don't know why I'm rushing so much just to make things hard for myself.
C: Why do you rush like that and suffer for it, I don't understand

standard: Vậy lỡ có bầu làm sao?
A: So what if she gets pregnant?
B: So what if I happen to get pregnant?
C: What if she accidentally gets pregnant?

standard: Hơi ấm ngày xưa vẫn còn đó, nhớ nhỉ?
A: The warmth of the past still lingers, doesn't it?
B: The warmth of the old days is still there, remember?
C: The warmth from the old days is still there, remember?

standard: Em tưởng hôm bữa là bạn đó chứ bạn này bây giờ em mới biết này chị.
A: I thought it was that friend from the other day, but I only just found out who this person is now, sister.
B: I thought it was that friend the other day, but I've only just found out about this friend now, sister.
C: I thought that was that friend from the other day, but this friend here, I only just found out about now, sis.

standard: làm tôi phải gọi điện mẹ xem thật không.
A: It made me call my mom to see if it was true.
B: It made me have to call my mother to see if it was true.
C: This makes me have to call mom to check if it's true.

standard: Chưa được chụp luôn ấy. Đợi bà ấy nhé
A: The photo hasn't been taken yet. Let's wait for her.
B: It hasn't even been taken yet. Wait for her.
C: It hasn't been taken yet. Wait for her.

standard: Bà bán ở đâu vậy admin, cho xin được cụ thể với ạ
A: Where does she sell her goods, admin? Could you please give me the specific location?
B: Where is the lady selling it, admin? Could you please provide the specific location?
C: Where do you sell, admin? Could you please give me the specific details?

standard: Lên thì dễ mà tí xuống không biết đường nào xuống.
A: Going up is easy, but coming down is a different story; you won't know how.
B: Going up is easy, but in a bit, I won't know which way to go down.
C: Going up is easy, but going down I don't know which way to go.

standard: Không thích nhau mà ở đâu cũng gặp vậy nhỉ?
A: Why do we keep running into each other everywhere when we don't like each other?
B: We don't like each other, so why do we keep running into each other everywhere?
C: We don't even like each other yet keep running into each other everywhere, huh?

standard: Bán ở đâu? Để khi nào về đi ăn một bữa nhé.
A: Where is it sold? Let's go have a meal there when I get back.
B: Where is it sold? Let's go eat there for a meal when I return.
C: Where is it sold? Let's go eat there when we get back.

standard: Nhà mình vẫn còn nguyên.
A: My house is still intact.
B: My house is still intact.
C: My house is still intact.

standard: Ê sau này tới tết em đi lên Châu Đốc này anh.
A: Hey, I'm going to Chau Doc next Tet, brother.
B: Hey, when Tet comes later, I'll go to Chau Doc.
C: Hey, I'll be going up to Chau Doc before Tet, bro.

standard: Bắt nhiều vậy mà không biết sợ là gì nhỉ?
A: They've arrested so many people, yet they don't seem to be afraid at all?
B: Catching so many, yet they don't seem to know what fear is, do they?
C: Getting caught this many times and still doesn't know what fear is?

standard: Mấy cô phơi lúa mà để gạch ra đường, cháu đi cháu bị vỡ miệng rồi.
A: Those women were drying rice and left bricks on the road; if I had walked by, I would have broken my mouth.
B: Those women were drying rice and left bricks in the road, and I hit them and broke my mouth.
C: Some of you ladies were drying rice but left bricks on the road, I walked by and split my mouth open already.

standard: Bánh cam nhưng củ sắn với dưa leo, xịt nước tương là ngon tuyệt vời.
A: Orange cake with cassava and cucumber, drizzled with soy sauce, is absolutely delicious.
B: Orange cakes but with jicama and cucumber, sprayed with soy sauce is absolutely wonderful.
C: Bánh cam with taro and cucumber, with soy sauce drizzled on top, is absolutely delicious.

standard: Cái gì vậy trời, chỗ nào cũng lụt hết nhỉ.
A: What the heck, everything's flooded everywhere!
B: What on earth, everywhere is flooded, isn't it?
C: Oh my god, it's flooded everywhere.

standard: Có một tí từ gần sáng đến giờ mà vậy đó chị.
A: It's been a little while since almost morning, that's why it's like this, sister.
B: It's just been a little bit from early morning until now, and that's how it is, sister.
C: Something's been going on since early morning, and it's still like that, chị.

standard: không ngon mà lại đắt nữa.
A: It's not tasty and it's expensive too.
B: It's not delicious and it's expensive too.
C: Not even good and expensive too.

standard: người quen kìa
A: That's an acquaintance!
B: Look, it's someone I know.
C: Look, there's someone I know.

standard: Tháng nào cũng đến, chứ cái này nghe khó đến.
A: It comes every month, but this one seems unlikely to happen.
B: It comes every month, but this one sounds hard to come by.
C: It comes every month, unlike this which sounds hard to come by.

standard: Anh không nhớ là cái bài “thú vị hơn vậy” đó nó hát bằng tiếng Nghệ sao nữa.
A: He couldn't remember if that "more interesting than that" song was sung in the Nghe An dialect.
B: I don't remember how that "more interesting than that" song is sung in the Nghe dialect anymore.
C: I can't remember anymore which song was called "more interesting than that" sung in the Nghệ dialect.

standard: chở người đẹp này qua bên đấy được này.
A: I can take this beautiful girl over there.
B: I can take this beauty over there.
C: Can I take this beautiful person over there?

standard: Mấy bà ơi ngon bà đắt!
A: Ladies, it's delicious but expensive!
B: Everyone, this is so delicious and worth every penny!
C: Ladies, it's delicious but pricey!

standard: Sao không thấy trong nhóm này là sao?
A: Why can't I see you in this group?
B: Why isn't it seen in this group?
C: Why can't I see it in this group?

standard: nhìn thèm quá, hôm nào ra mua
A: It looks so tempting, I'll go buy some sometime.
B: It looks so delicious, I'll go buy some one of these days.
C: I really want this, I'll go buy it one of these days

standard: nghe có gì sai sai, đại gia quận Cam gì lạ vậy
A: Something sounds off here, what kind of strange Orange County tycoon is he?
B: Something sounds off, what kind of strange "Orange District tycoon" is this?
C: Something sounds a bit off, what's so weird about this rich person from Orange County

standard: Cá heo, cá này mà đem kho tiêu cho cay rồi luộc đậu bắp chấm ăn là tuyệt vời
A: Dolphin fish, braised with spicy peppers and served with boiled okra as a dipping sauce, is absolutely delicious.
B: Dolphins; if this fish is braised with pepper to make it spicy and eaten with boiled okra for dipping, it would be wonderful.
C: Dolphin, if we braise this fish with pepper to make it spicy and then boil okra for dipping, it would be amazing

standard: Chúng tôi là vậy đấy.
A: That's just how we are.
B: That's just how we are.
C: We are like that.

standard: Ở nhà là không thoát đâu nhỉ?
A: Staying at home won't help, right?
B: There's no escaping it even at home, right?
C: There's no escaping being at home, is there?

standard: Phải chan thật nhiều mới ngon.
A: It tastes best when you pour a lot of it over the dish.
B: You have to pour plenty of broth for it to be delicious.
C: You have to add a lot to make it delicious.

standard: bài ca muôn thuở, nói thôi chứ có thực hiện đâu
A: It's the same old story, all talk, no action.
B: The same old story; they just talk but never actually do it.
C: Same old empty promises - just talking but never actually following through

standard: mày làm gì xấu hổ vậy em
A: What are you doing that's so embarrassing, girl?
B: What are you doing that's so embarrassing?
C: What embarrassing thing did you do?

standard: làm 1 cân rượu chắc là đủ nhỉ?
A: Making one pound of liquor should be enough, right?
B: Making 1 kilo of wine should be enough, right?
C: Making 1 kilogram of wine should probably be enough, right?

standard: Không phải mỗi sữa này đâu.
A: It's not just this milk.
B: It's not just this milk.
C: It's not just this milk.

standard: Cho đi cái chỗ đó với
A: Give that place away.
B: Please send me the location of that place.
C: Just let go of that place

standard: Cái này tao viết từ bữa nào rồi
A: I wrote this a while ago.
B: I wrote this a while ago.
C: When did I write this?

standard: Nhà trong này nghe nói đắt
A: I heard that houses in this area are expensive.
B: I heard that houses in here are expensive.
C: I heard the houses in here are expensive

standard: Ai làm mâm này giơ tay lên xem.
A: Raise your hand if you made this platter.
B: Whoever made this platter, raise your hand.
C: Who made this platter, raise your hand and show me.

standard: mai nói bố kéo màn rồi vào nhậu đỡ muỗi.
A: Mai told Dad to pull the curtains shut so they could go inside and have a drink to avoid the mosquitoes.
B: Tomorrow, tell Dad to pull the mosquito net and then come in to drink so we can avoid the mosquitoes.
C: Tell Dad tomorrow to pull the screen and then come drink to avoid mosquitoes.

standard: đang chụp hình hay sao mà không thấy nhỉ?
A: Are they taking pictures or something? I can't see them.
B: Are you taking photos or something? Why don't I see you?
C: Are we taking photos or what? Why can't I see anything?

standard: hẹn mấy anh em chơi hết mình tại sự kiện nhé.
A: Let's all have a blast at the event!
B: I'll see you guys and let's give it our all at the event.
C: Meeting up with my buddies to have a blast at the event. See you there!

standard: làm cái gì ngoài đó vậy anh
A: What are you doing out there, man?
B: What are you doing out there?
C: What are you doing out there?
